text:
There once was a peculiar old house in the woods, shrouded in twilight. This wasn't just any house. It had been lived in once by a man who kept to himself, obsessed with antique clocks. The floors creaked with every step, and the walls whispered secrets of distant times. Inside was his sanctuary—a library of clocks from every era.

One evening, as the chill settled into the thickwooded glen, a stranger arrived. He was cloaked, making him indistinguishable from shadows. Intrigued, the old man invited him in. That man turned out to be an expert on clocks, someone who traveled far and wide to find rare specimens.

Their conversations were intense and late into the night. The old man regaled his guest with stories of his collection's rarest ticking anomalies, while the stranger offered insights into clocks that needed saving.

After days spent in each other's company, their discussions shifted. The newcomer became fascinated with the intricacies of time
